fixtures.description: Geometric countable-support belief with a computable tail bound and threshold-triggered commitment update.
Poisson countable-support model with certified tail approximants and bounded observable expectations.
Countable-state computable Markov kernel with finite-horizon conditioning and revision history.
A finite support truncation is asserted to be the exact infinite-support distribution and drops certified tail mass.
The representation invokes an undeclared exact infinite-sum oracle or future random-outcome oracle.
Conditioning is requested on a null or effectively undecidable zero-probability event without a declared regular conditional interface.
The source measure or transition kernel is noncomputable and supplies no effective approximation interface.
An unbounded observable is queried without a certified integrability or convergence modulus.
